title: B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
outcome: The importer starts the CHED-PP clone flow from Your import notifications, picks the 'Plants, plant products and other objects' (CHEDPP) certificate type, searches for phytosanitary certificate PHYTO.FR.2026.0000064 (France, issued 15/04/2026, party 'WorldUSIN74 Co'), and the app responds with a 'You cannot clone this certificate' page instead of the clone summary — the expected 'Clone' button never renders and the test times out after 30s, so no notification is ever submitted despite the test title.

error.message: locator.click: Timeout 30000ms exceeded. waiting for getByRole('button', { name: 'Clone' })
error.rootCause: Search succeeded but routed to the 'Unable to clone' page, not the clone summary. The Clone button does not exist on that page.

notes: Trace metadata reports 48 actions / 16 pages; the actions timeline lists 33 entries of which 12 are real user actions (the rest are Before/After Hooks, fixtures and worker cleanup, excluded per instructions).

The clone-summary page (with the Clone button) and everything downstream of it — the cloned CHED-PP notification form and its submission — were NEVER exercised in this trace. That surface is a coverage gap, not an absence of requirement.
The cloning entry point is a persistent 'Clone a certificate' link on Your import notifications.
Cloning is keyed on four search inputs: certificate type, country of origin, certificate reference, date of issue and party name. The reference format observed is PHYTO.<country>.<year>.<serial>.

The app has a defined graceful-degradation path when a certificate lacks the data needed to clone: offer a PDF download and a 'Create a new notification with attachment' route rather than blocking the user.
Persistent service chrome on every page: Address book, Manage account, Manage trade partners, Sign out; signed-in user 'Michael Scott'; Beta banner with feedback link.

ARCHITECTURE — DO NOT COPY: IPAFFS answers the clone refusal with HTTP 406 Not Acceptable on GET /cloning/summary. 406 is a content-negotiation status; using it for a business-rule outcome ('this certificate lacks required data') is a status-code misuse. The new CDP app should render this outcome as a normal 200 outcome page.

The cloning search is POST-redirect-GET: POST /cloning/search -> 302 -> GET /cloning/summary?ehcRef=..&origin=..&certificateDateOfIssue=..&partyName=..&certType=... The whole composite lookup key travels in the query string rather than in session state — worth noting as a deliberate statelessness choice the new app could keep.

The PDF fallback is keyed by the same composite: GET /cloning/pdf?ehcRef=..&origin=..&certType=..&issueDate=..&partyName=... Note the param is 'issueDate' here but 'certificateDateOfIssue' on /cloning/summary — inconsistent naming for the same value across two endpoints.
Page URLs confirmed from the trace network log (requests 4238, 4254, 4271, 4288), all under host importnotification-static-snd.azure.defra.cloud with the /notification/vnet prefix.

The trace's 4303 requests include a great deal of setup traffic (traders / review / declaration / confirmation / bip-notifications sequences) that belongs to other pages in the same context and is NOT attributable to this test's own 12 actions. Do not read that traffic as evidence of what this journey exercised.
